Original: Java

phase1.explanation:
Strings are objects (not primitives) that represent text:

Key methods:
• length() — returns number of characters
• charAt(index) — gets character at position
• substring(start, end) — extracts portion
• toUpperCase() / toLowerCase() — case conversion
• trim() — removes leading/trailing spaces
• contains(substring) — checks if substring exists
• equals(other) — compares content (not reference!)

Important: Strings are IMMUTABLE!
• Methods return NEW strings, don't modify the original
• Use equals() NOT == for comparing content

⚠️ Very Common Mistake: Using == instead of .equals() for Strings

Pro tip: Use .equalsIgnoreCase() for case-insensitive comparison.

phase2.errorMessage:
They are NOT equal!

(No error — but the logic is WRONG! str1 and str2 have the same content but == says they're different.)
phase2.explanation:
This is the String Trap! 

== checks if str1 and str2 point to the SAME memory location.
str1 and str2 are different objects, so == returns false even though the content is the same.

Always use .equals() for comparing String content:
str1.equals(str2) // true ✅

For case-insensitive: str1.equalsIgnoreCase(str2)

phase3.prompt:
Write a program that:
1. Declares a String: "  Java is Fun  "
2. Prints its length
3. Prints it trimmed
4. Prints it in uppercase
5. Prints the character at index 5
6. Prints the substring from index 2 to 6
dialogueHints: Use the trim() method to remove spaces at both ends
phase3.openingDialogue: Strings are your friends — they hold all the text! 📝
